page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-96 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8581C processor. It features 96 virtual CPUs mapped to 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The system is equipped with 744.0 GB of RAM, yielding 7.75 GB of memory per core. Cache resources include 48 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and a large 260 MB L3 cache. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or bundled GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core capabilities and memory throughput. The server achieved top-tier results in Geekbench single-core and multi-core evaluations, PassMark CPU score, and static web serving throughput. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and image processing also placed in the top tier. In contrast, single-core stress-ng performance and PassMark memory scores fell into the average category. Cryptographic operations performed exceptionally well, with the PassMark CPU encryption test ranking in the top tier, aided by hardware-accelerated instruction sets like AES and VAES.

The Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions and Advanced Matrix Extensions. These hardware accelerators contribute to strong performance in machine learning workloads. LLM inference benchmarks show top-tier prompt processing speeds for small and medium models, though text generation speeds for larger models drop to average levels.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-highmem-96 offers high memory density and compute capacity without the added cost of bundled GPUs or local storage, making it a specialized choice for memory-bound workloads. However, the lack of local SSD storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may introduce latency tradeoffs. This server is suited for memory-intensive database operations, large-scale software compilation, text processing, and CPU-based LLM prompt processing.
description: The Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-96 is an x86_64 server configured with 96 vCPUs and 744.0 GB of RAM, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz. It features 48 physical cores, a 260 MB L3 cache, and lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks place this server in the top tier for Geekbench multi-core, static web serving, and image processing, while single-core stress-ng and PassMark memory scores remain average. The processor includes Advanced Matrix Extensions and AVX-512, which drive strong prompt processing speeds in LLM inference. Qualitatively, the instance offers high resource density for memory-intensive applications, though the absence of local storage represents a key architectural tradeoff. It is suited for database operations, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-96 features 96 vCPUs, 744 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C processor. It delivers top-tier multi-core performance and is optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-96 is an x86_64 server with 96 vCPUs and 744 GB RAM, optimized for memory-intensive database and compute workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory and compute platform featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and advanced matrix extensions for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 744 GB RAM powered by Intel Xeon 8581C processors
Advanced Matrix Extensions for accelerated CPU-based LLM inference
Top-tier performance in multi-core benchmarks and static web serving
No local storage or GPU resources, requiring network-attached storage
Suited for large-scale databases, software compilation, and text processing
